# Research - 2026-07-04

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> 1-day delta. Saturday: weekly repos skipped. Prior #1 (exec-flag)
> LANDED as PR #909 + #910. Prior #2 (AbortSignal) + #3
> (`stripInternalOptions`) still open. Two NEW items from cline
> #12061 + #12057 dominate.

## Executive Summary

- **#1 (~3h, NEW)**: Port cline #12061 leniency. `agenticChat.ts:225`
  does `JSON.parse(toolCall.function.arguments)` and hard-fails when a
  weak model closes a brace late or single-quotes a string, burning the
  turn.
- **#2 (~2h, NEW)**: Port cline #12057. Our `switchAgent` (code / plan)
  is a silent session-level flip; the model sees a new system prompt
  but no in-message marker, so recency bias keeps it acting under the
  old regime.
- **#3 (~2h, RE-SURFACED)**: `sendChat` (`orchestrator.ts:78`) still
  discards `AbortSignal`; non-streaming burns tokens after Ctrl+C.
- **#4 (~1h, RE-SURFACED)**: `stripInternalOptions` (`agent/index.ts:50`)
  still uncalled; wire into agenticChat + streamingOrchestrator before
  options reach `provider.complete()`.
- **Deferred**: opencode #35185 / #35085 (new `@opencode-ai/codemode`
  package), #35113 / #35190 (cosmetic TUI), cline #12058 (folded into
  #2), #12054 / #12056 (our agents are user-driven not tool-invoked),
  kilocode #11887 (no standalone daemon), #11923 (different permission
  layer), #11918 (VS Code RTL only). **Rotation**: no changes.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Lenient tool-call argument parsing + shaped schema errors (tools, ~3h)

- **What**: In `src/core/agenticChat.ts:222-238`, replace the single
  `JSON.parse` with (a) fast path, (b) on syntax error run a local
  repair (strip trailing commas, close missing braces/brackets by
  counting, unwrap single-quoted strings) before returning `Invalid
  JSON`. On Zod errors caught at line 251, format `err.issues[0].path` +
  `.message` into the error text so the model can self-correct. No new
  dep: a ~40-line recovery covers the two classes cline saw
  (`{"commands":"ls"}`, `{"files":[{"path":"a.txt"}]` unterminated).
  Tests: trailing-comma parses; singular `bash.command` returns shaped
  error; well-formed JSON hits fast path.
- **Why**: Weak / cheap models routinely emit malformed args. Today one
  fumble burns the whole turn and the loop retries with no signal about
  what to fix. cline's telemetry shows `tool_call_invalid_json` +
  `tool_call_type_validation` as the two largest raw buckets under
  `provider_api_error`.
- **Source**: cline/cline#12061 (`a1d69fe`, 2026-07-04): drops the strict
  AI-SDK validator and adds `experimental_repairToolCall`.
- **Category**: tools. **Size**: small (~3h).

#### 2. Stamp agent switches into the outbound user message (agent, ~2h)

- **What**: On `AgentRegistry.switchTo` (`agent/index.ts:197`), record a
  pending switch on the session. In the next `sendChat` / `agenticChat`
  call, prepend `<agent_switch from="X" to="Y"/>` to the first user
  turn after the switch. Add `stripInternalWrappers` (TUI + `sessions
  export`) so it never reaches the user. Add a two-line paragraph to
  each `agent/prompts/*.txt` explaining switch markers denote a
  mid-conversation regime change (newest wins). Round-trip cancels.
  Tests: switch then send -> tag on wire; double-switch -> no tag;
  rendered transcript hides tag + reminder.
- **Why**: Today `switchAgent` swaps the system prompt on the next
  build. Models do not diff system prompts across turns; the prior
  regime's tool traces dominate recency. cline's fix landed for the
  same bug class (plan / act) with the same two-piece design, and we
  have exactly that gap between `code` and `plan`.
- **Source**: cline/cline#12057 (`b5468e1`, 2026-07-03) + follow-up fix
  #12058 (`3575b38`) to stop `normalizeUserInput` from stripping the
  tag before it reaches the wire.
- **Category**: agent. **Size**: small (~2h).

#### 3. Thread AbortSignal through non-streaming sendChat (provider, ~2h)

- **What**: Add `signal?: AbortSignal` to `sendChat` options
  (`orchestrator.ts:6-14`), forward to `provider.complete`. Wire
  `interactive.ts` Ctrl+C to an `AbortController` mirroring streaming.
  Distinguish `AbortError` in `router.ts` so aborts are not recorded as
  `permanent`. Tests: abort throws promptly; route health unchanged.
- **Why**: Streaming honors Ctrl+C; non-streaming keeps burning tokens
  after cancel. `sapOrchestration.ts:320` already accepts `signal`.
- **Source**: prior `2026-07-03-research.md:60` + sst/opencode#34838
  (`39dfbb5`, 2026-07-02).
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: small (~2h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Order**: #1 (~3h, fewer failed turns on cheap models), #2 (~2h,
   trustworthy mid-conversation switches), #3 (~2h, AbortSignal gap),
   #4 (~1h, trivial wire-up left from 2026-07-03).
2. **Do NOT** carry cline's strict-validator drop verbatim: we do not
   use the Vercel AI SDK. The analog is our own `JSON.parse` + Zod
   `parse` seams. Keep the fix local to `agenticChat.ts` and each
   tool's Zod schema; do not add `jsonrepair` as a hard dep unless the
   local ~40-line recovery proves insufficient in tests.
3. **No demotions**: daily set all produced actionable signal today.
   Weekly repos (aider, claude-code) resume Monday 2026-07-06.
